The latest announcement is out from Victory Goldfields Limited ( (AU:VTM) ).

Victory Metals Limited has notified the market of the issue of 200,000 unquoted performance rights, effective 29 April 2026. The new securities, which are not listed on the ASX, form part of the company’s broader capital management and incentive framework, potentially aligning management and staff interests more closely with shareholder value over time.

The issuance of additional performance rights marginally increases Victory Metals’ pool of unquoted equity instruments, but does not immediately affect the number of quoted shares on issue. For investors, the move signals continued reliance on equity-based remuneration to retain and motivate personnel as the company progresses its metals projects, with any future conversion of these rights potentially resulting in some equity dilution.

More about Victory Goldfields Limited

Victory Metals Limited is an Australian-listed resources company focused on metals exploration and development. The company is listed on the ASX under the ticker VTM and pursues growth through advancing its portfolio of mineral projects, supported by equity-based incentives for key personnel.

Megado Gold Ltd. ( (AU:MEG) ) has provided an announcement.

Megado Minerals Ltd has notified the market that 7,000,000 options with an exercise price of $0.10, expiring on 28 April 2026 under the code MEGAR, have lapsed unexercised. The cessation of these options marginally reduces the company’s potential future share dilution, simplifying its capital structure but without an immediate impact on its existing issued capital.

The announcement, lodged as an Appendix 3H on 29 April 2026, formally updates the Australian Securities Exchange on changes to Megado’s securities. While primarily administrative, the move clarifies the status of outstanding options for investors and may slightly adjust expectations around future equity issuance and capital management flexibility.

More about Megado Gold Ltd.

Megado Minerals Ltd, listed on the ASX under the code MEG, operates in the minerals and mining sector. The company’s activities center on resource exploration and development, positioning it within Australia’s broader mining and commodities industry.

IGO ( (AU:IGO) ) has provided an update.

IGO Limited has announced the resignation of its Chief Financial Officer, Johan van Vuuren, effective 29 April 2026, after both parties agreed the role was not the right fit for his career aspirations. The company has begun arranging interim CFO coverage and will run a process to appoint a permanent replacement, with the CEO emphasising the strength of the existing finance team and a focus on ensuring a smooth leadership transition.

The change in financial leadership introduces a period of transition for IGO’s executive ranks, but the company’s swift move to secure interim cover and identify a new CFO aims to limit disruption to its operations and financial stewardship. Stakeholders will be watching for the forthcoming update on the recruitment process, as the next CFO appointment will be important for supporting IGO’s strategic and capital allocation decisions in a competitive resources market.

data.title: New Zealand Rural Land Co. Releases 2025 Climate-Related Disclosures Report
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

New Zealand Rural Land Co. Ltd. ( (NZ:NZL) ) has shared an update.

New Zealand Rural Land Company Limited has released its 2025 Climate-Related Disclosures Report, outlining how climate change factors into its governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics and targets. The report frames climate as a financially material issue for the long-term value and resilience of its rural land portfolio, and details how diversification across land uses and regions, tenant engagement, and initiatives such as forestry expansion and native regeneration support climate resilience and value preservation.

The disclosure includes climate scenario analysis, identification of climate-related risks and opportunities, and a greenhouse gas inventory for the year ended 31 December 2025. It highlights priority decarbonisation and resilience activities, including land-use positioning, carbon removals, and adaptation to physical climate risks, signalling an ongoing integration of climate considerations into capital allocation and portfolio development decisions.

More about New Zealand Rural Land Co. Ltd.

New Zealand Rural Land Company Limited is a rural land investment business focused on building and managing a diversified portfolio of high-quality agricultural and forestry assets across New Zealand. The company owns more than 17,000 hectares of rural land, which it leases under long-term arrangements, positioning itself as a landowner rather than an operator and influencing outcomes through land selection, land-use mix, and tenant relationships.

SIV Capital Limited ( (AU:SIV) ) has shared an update.

SIV Capital Limited has reported modest operating cash inflows from customers and interest income for the quarter ended 31 March 2026, but these were outweighed by legal and other administrative costs. As a result, the company recorded a net operating cash outflow of A$86,000 for the quarter and A$311,000 year-to-date, while registering no cash movements from investing or financing activities, underscoring a period of limited capital deployment and a continued emphasis on managing overheads.

The cash flow profile indicates that SIV Capital is currently sustaining its operations primarily through existing cash resources and interest income rather than new investments or borrowings. This pattern may signal a cautious financial stance or a transitional phase in which the company is focused on controlling expenses, with stakeholders likely to monitor how prolonged outflows and the absence of growth-oriented spending could affect future liquidity and strategic flexibility.

More about SIV Capital Limited

SIV Capital Limited is an ASX-listed company, but the provided filing offers no description of its industry, principal activities, or core products and services. As the document is a standard Appendix 4C quarterly cash flow report, it focuses solely on financial metrics rather than operational or market details, leaving the company’s specific market focus unidentified.

data.title: Stavely Minerals Posts Continued Cash Outflows as Exploration Funding Relies on External Capital
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Stavely Minerals Ltd ( (AU:SVY) ) just unveiled an update.

Stavely Minerals reported a quarterly cash outflow from operating activities of A$438,000 for the March 2026 quarter, driven mainly by exploration, exploration staff, and corporate expenses, against modest receipts and interest income. The company recorded minimal investing activity aside from a small cash bond release, while financing inflows, including prior equity raising and borrowings, helped limit the net decrease in cash, leaving A$471,000 in cash and equivalents at period end.

Over the nine months to March 2026, cumulative operating cash outflows reached A$2.915 million, partially offset by A$1.955 million of net financing inflows, underscoring Stavely Minerals’ reliance on external capital to fund its exploration program. The cash burn profile highlights the ongoing funding needs and typical risk-return dynamics faced by exploration-focused miners, with liquidity management remaining a key consideration for shareholders and potential investors.

More about Stavely Minerals Ltd

Stavely Minerals Limited is an Australian mining exploration company focused on mineral discovery and evaluation activities. The company generates limited operating receipts and primarily deploys capital into exploration, staff, and corporate costs typical of early-stage resource explorers rather than revenue-generating production operations.

data.title: ACC stake in KMD Brands diluted after retail entitlement offer
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from KMD Brands Limited ( (NZ:KMD) ) is now available.

KMD Brands Limited, a dual-listed outdoor and lifestyle apparel group, operates primarily through branded retail and wholesale channels across New Zealand, Australia and other markets. Its core business centres on designing and selling clothing and equipment aimed at travel, adventure and outdoor-focused consumers.

Accident Compensation Corporation has disclosed that its substantial holding in KMD Brands has been diluted from 11.628% to 9.355% following the issue of 351,832,889 new shares under the retail component of KMD’s underwritten accelerated renounceable entitlement offer. ACC did not participate in the retail tranche, having only taken part in the institutional component, so its absolute shareholding is unchanged but now represents a smaller proportion of KMD’s expanded capital base, modestly reshaping the company’s ownership structure for existing shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (NZ:KMD) stock is a Buy with a N$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KMD Brands Limited stock, see the NZ:KMD Stock Forecast page.

More about KMD Brands Limited

KMD Brands Limited is a listed retailer and brand owner in the outdoor and lifestyle apparel sector, with its ordinary shares quoted on both the NZX and ASX. The company targets consumers in New Zealand, Australia and other markets through its portfolio of brands, focusing on clothing and equipment for travel, adventure and everyday outdoor use.

Kapstream Investment Trust ( (AU:KIT) ) has issued an announcement.

Kapstream Investment Trust has released an unaudited weekly estimate of its net tangible asset backing, reporting an NTA of $2.0086 per unit as at 27 April 2026. The update offers investors a timely indicator of the trust’s underlying portfolio value and supports transparency for unitholders monitoring performance and market pricing.

The NTA disclosure, authorised by the responsible entity Equity Trustees, reinforces ongoing reporting practices typical of listed investment vehicles on the ASX. Regular publication of these figures can influence investor sentiment and trading activity in KIT units, as they provide a reference point for assessing whether the units trade at a premium or discount to asset value.
